event: born in a country with a high burden of tuberculosis (TB)
in the care of a tertiary care infectious disease clinic for TB infection (TBI) based on a positive QuantiFERON gold test
had not taken corticosteroids for several years
partially empty sella turcica on magnetic resonance imaging
began treatment with 600 mg rifampin (10 mg/kg/d)
ambulance transport to the emergency department
admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
no rash or mucous membrane involvement
blood film did not show schistocytes
computed tomography (CT) scan of the head normal
CT angiography of the neck: no infected thrombus
CT angiography of the neck: no edematous retropharyngeal soft tissue
CT angiography of the neck: localized myositis
CT imaging of the lungs: bilateral increased septal thickening with ground glass opacities
rifampin dose administered in the ICU
avoid rifamycin-based medications in the future
